faq.question: How much does window replacement cost in Alameda?
faq.answer: Most Alameda homeowners replacing windows throughout their home invest between $7,000 and $16,000 depending on window count, frame material, and glass package. Victorian and Craftsman homes along Grand Street and the West End often require custom-sized frames to match original profiles, which adds to cost. Single windows start around $600 installed; full-home projects for historic homes can reach $20,000+.

faq.question: Do I need a permit for window replacement in Alameda?
faq.answer: Yes. The City of Alameda requires a building permit for window replacement projects. Homes located within historic overlay districts — such as those near the Park Street or Webster Street corridors — may also require review by the Historical Advisory Board to ensure replacements are compatible with the property's historic character. Our team manages all permit applications on your behalf.

faq.question: Which window brands does Deluxe Windows install in Alameda?
faq.answer: In Alameda we most frequently install Milgard Ultra fiberglass for its salt-air resistance and dimensional stability — critical for island homes exposed to Bay moisture. We also install Andersen 400-Series wood-clad for Victorian and Craftsman homes where interior wood aesthetics matter, and Anlin Del Mar for homeowners prioritizing maximum energy efficiency and UV protection.

faq.question: Does Deluxe Windows serve all Alameda neighborhoods?
faq.answer: Yes — we serve all Alameda neighborhoods including the Gold Coast, West End, Bay Farm Island, Harbor Bay Isle, and the Fernside district. We cover all Alameda zip codes: 94501 and 94502.
